texto: ...today, I’m writing about the extraordinary; those ‘moments’ in life that reach us, and change us. Those moments take place all the time, really – it’s just that we’re taught not to notice. We’re taught that whims are for the foolish; that instinct and passion are more akin to fairy tales than reality; that they won’t get us very far, and everyone would laugh at us if they knew what we really held close to our hearts.It’s a lie we allow ourselves, and it’s understandable.But it’s simply not true.

texto: God,” she butted her head into his chest, “I'm so angry with myself.”“What? explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Dianna Hardy que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “How can you lose me? You’ve owned me from the first moment I saw you.”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amizade, belong, belonging, com foco em belonging e bond.
